_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Traumatic peripheral vascular injury (penetrating trauma, blunt trauma) requiring repair
Vascular injuries manifesting as laceration, transection, intimal disruption, intimal flaps, or focal wall defects requiring repair
Vascular injuries causing massive hemorrhage or limb necrosis due to tissue and organ ischemia, necessitating immediate hemorrhage control and rapid restoration of blood flow via repair

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes - detailed history, signs & symptoms, indication for procedure
Was the duplex USG report submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed Procedure / Operative notes submitted?
Is there a Detailed Discharge Summary mentioning date of follow-up submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes not suggestive of peripheral arterial injury
Clinical notes are incomplete or do not clearly indicate the need for peripheral arterial injury repair
Duplex USG report is missing or inadequate
Detailed Procedure / Operative notes are missing or insufficient
Detailed Discharge Summary is missing or does not include follow-up date

clinical_key_pointers: Traumatic peripheral vascular injury is a significant cause of disability and death either in civilian environments or on the battlefield.
Penetrating trauma and blunt trauma are the most common forms of vascular injuries.
Besides, iatrogenic arterial injury (IAI) is another pattern of vascular trauma.
The management of peripheral vascular injuries has been improved in different environments and wars.
surgical_options: vascular reconstruction with blood vessel prostheses or vascular grafts
Surgical operation is still the primary treatment for vascular injuries
